volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 1.2.156b - Section 1.2.202b)

sections.label: Section 1.2.156b
sections.translation: Ah ! your words , upon my faith , are increasing my weakness . You are worrying me to death !

sections.label: Section 1.2.158
sections.translation: What , is this the way you ’ re obedient to me ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.158b
sections.translation: What do you want me to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.158c
sections.translation: What , you ? What I want is this —

sections.label: Section 1.2.159
sections.original: Quid id est igitur quod vis ?
sections.translation: What is it then that you do want ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.159b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.159c
sections.translation: Tell me , then .

sections.label: Section 1.2.160
sections.original: Dormientis spectatores metuis ne ex somno excites ?
sections.translation: Are you afraid lest you should wake the drowsy Spectators [*] from their nap ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.161
sections.translation: Woe be to you !

sections.label: Section 1.2.161b
sections.original: Tibi equidem a portu adporto hoc —
sections.translation: For my part , that same am I bringing to you from the harbour .

sections.label: Section 1.2.161c
sections.translation: What are you bringing ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.162
sections.original: Vim metum , cruciatum curam , iurgiumque atque inopiam .
sections.translation: Violence , alarm , torture , care , strife , and beggary .

sections.label: Section 1.2.163
sections.original: Perii , tu quidem thensaurum huc mi adportavisti mali .
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! You really are bringing me hither a store of evils . I ’ m ruined outright .

sections.label: Section 1.2.164b
sections.translation: Why , yes , you are —

sections.label: Section 1.2.164c
sections.translation: I know it already ; you ’ ll be saying I ’ m wretched .

sections.label: Section 1.2.164d
sections.translation: ’ Tis you have said so ; I ’ m mum .

sections.label: Section 1.2.165
sections.translation: What mishap is this ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.165b
sections.translation: Don ’ t enquire . It is a very great calamity .

sections.label: Section 1.2.166
sections.original: Obsecro , dissolve iam me ; nimis diu animi pendeo .
sections.translation: Prithee , do relieve me at once . Too long a time have I been in suspense .

sections.label: Section 1.2.167
sections.original: Placide , multa exquirere etiam prius volo quam vapulem .
sections.translation: Softly ; I still wish to make many enquiries before I ’ m beaten .

sections.label: Section 1.2.168
sections.original: Hercle vero vapulabis , nisi iam loquere aut hinc abis .
sections.translation: By my troth , you assuredly will be beaten , unless you say at once , or get away from here .

sections.label: Section 1.2.169
sections.original: Hoc sis vide , ut palpatur . nullust , quando occepit , blandior .
sections.translation: Do look at that , please , how he does coax me ; there ’ s no one more flattering when he sets about it .

sections.label: Section 1.2.170
sections.original: Obsecro hercle oroque ut istuc quid sit actutum indices ,
sections.translation: By heavens , I do entreat and beseech you to disclose to me at once what it is ; inasmuch as I see that I must be the suppliant of my own servant .

sections.label: Section 1.2.172
sections.translation: And do I seem so unworthy of it ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.172b
sections.translation: Oh no , quite worthy .

sections.label: Section 1.2.172c
sections.translation: Well , so I thought .

sections.label: Section 1.2.173
sections.translation: Prithee , is the ship lost ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.173b
sections.translation: The ship ’ s all right ; don ’ t fear about that .

sections.label: Section 1.2.174
sections.translation: Well then , the rest of the cargo ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.174b
sections.translation: That ’ s right and tight .

sections.label: Section 1.2.174c
sections.translation: Why then don ’ t you tell

sections.label: Section 1.2.175
sections.original: quid siet quod me per urbem currens quaerebas modo .
sections.translation: me what it is , for which , just now , running through the city , you were seeking me ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.176
sections.original: Tu quidem ex óre orationem mi eripis .
sections.translation: Really , you are taking the words out of my mouth .

sections.label: Section 1.2.176b
sections.translation: I ’ ll hold my tongue .

sections.label: Section 1.2.176c
sections.translation: Do hold your tongue . I doubt , if I brought you any good news , you ’ d be dreadfully pressing , who are now insisting upon my speaking out , when you must hear bad news .

sections.label: Section 1.2.179
sections.original: Obsecro hercle te , istuc ut tu mihi malum facias palam .
sections.translation: Troth then , prithee do you let me know what this misfortune is .

sections.label: Section 1.2.180
sections.original: Eloquar , quandoquidem me oras . tuos pater —
sections.translation: Since you beg of me , I ’ ll tell you . Your father —

sections.label: Section 1.2.180b
sections.translation: My father did what ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.181
sections.translation: Your mistress —

sections.label: Section 1.2.181b
sections.translation: What about her ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.181c
sections.translation: He has seen her .

sections.label: Section 1.2.181d
sections.translation: Seen her ? Ah wretch that I am ! What I ask you , answer me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.182b
sections.original: Quin tu , si quid vis , roga .
sections.translation: Nay , but do you ask me , if you want anything .

sections.label: Section 1.2.183
sections.translation: How could he see her ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.183b
sections.translation: With his eyes .

sections.label: Section 1.2.183c
sections.translation: In what way ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.183d
sections.translation: Wide open .

sections.label: Section 1.2.184
sections.original: In hinc dierectus ? núgare in re capitali mea .
sections.translation: Away hence and be hanged . You are trifling , when my life ’ s at stake .

sections.label: Section 1.2.185
sections.original: Qui , malum , ego nugor , si tibi quod me rogas respondeo ?
sections.translation: How the plague am I trifling , if I answer you what you ask me ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.186
sections.translation: Did he see her for certain ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.186b
sections.original: Tam hercle certe quám ego te aut tu me vides .
sections.translation: Aye , troth , as certainly as I see you and you see me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.187
sections.translation: Where did he see her ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.187b
sections.original: Intus intra navem , út prope astitit ;
sections.translation: Down on board the ship , as he stood near the prow and chatted with her .

sections.label: Section 1.2.188b
sections.translation: Father , you have undone me . Come now , you , come now , you sir ? Why , you whip-rascal , didn ’ t you take care that he mightn ’ t see her ? Why , villain , didn ’ t you stow her away , that my father mightn ’ t perceive her ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.191
sections.original: Quia negotiosi eramus nos nostris negotiis :
sections.translation: Because we were busily employed about our business ; we were engaged in packing up and arranging the cargo . While these things were being done , your father was brought alongside in a very small boat ; and not an individual beheld the man until he was aboard the ship .

sections.label: Section 1.2.195
sections.original: Nequiquam , mare , subterfugi á tuis tempestatibus :
sections.translation: In vain have I escaped the sea with its dreadful tempests ! Just now I really did suppose that I was both ashore and in a place of safety ; but I see that by the raging waves I am being hurried towards the rocks . Say on ; what took place ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.198b
sections.translation: After he espied the woman , he began to ask her to whom she belonged .

sections.label: Section 1.2.199_200b
sections.translation: What did she answer ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.199_200c
sections.translation: That instant I ran up and interposed , saying that you had bought her as a maid-servant for your mother .

sections.label: Section 1.2.202b
sections.translation: Did he seem to believe you in that ?
